title: Recording AIS
text: AISStream.io delivers AIS (vessel-tracking) messages single WebSocket. Two properties feed dictate client must behave: must subscribe within three seconds connecting, server closes socket. subscription one JSON frame naming bounding boxes (optional filters). server watches TCP read queue closes connection backs . whole-world feed runs roughly 300 messages second. handler real work per message — parsing JSON, building data.table, touching database — read queue grows get dropped. aisstream answers . first handled : AisStream re-sends subscription every (re)connect via connectcore’s .resubscribe() seam, 3-second deadline always met, including reconnect. second shapes whole design — message hot path never parses. .dispatch() emits frame raw string cheap prefix check split error frames. practical consequence: don’t parse live handler. Record raw frames now, parse offline. exactly ndjson_sink() .

title: Recording AIS
text: ndjson_sink(dir) returns "message" handler — function(raw) — register Node-ws style, just like connectcore::ws_file_sink(). Wire , drive loop $run(): , part matters: Opens hourly file first frame, appends raw frame one-line write — minimum work, socket drains fast enough server never drops . One JSON frame per line (NDJSON). Rolls flushes message , separate timer. turn UTC hour handler closes current file opens next (ais-YYYY-MM-DDTHH.ndjson); flush_seconds elapsed flushes buffer disk. Writes append-, crash costs last unflushed window, final partial buffer flushes process exit. Files named UTC hour, ndjson_hour_path() lets predict path:
code: ais <- AisStream$new(   # AISSTREAM_API_KEY is read from the environment by default   bounding_boxes = list(list(min_lat = -90, min_lon = -180, max_lat = 90, max_lon = 180)),   message_types = "PositionReport" )  ais$on("message", ndjson_sink(dir = "ais-data", flush_seconds = 5)) ais$run() # blocks, pumping the event loop; Ctrl-C to stop ndjson_hour_path("ais-data", at = lubridate::ymd_hms("2024-03-15 09:42:11", tz = "UTC")) #> [1] "ais-data/ais-2024-03-15T09.ndjson"

title: Recording AIS
text: recorded, parse leisure — JSON work belongs. Read file line line turn frame tidy row: parse_ais() deliberately generic (JSON.parse AIS): model 25 message bodies, AISStream API beta bodies shift. flatteners (ais_metadata(), as_position_report()) model stable common fields, parse_go_time() handles Go-format time_utc (ISO 8601) — note sub-second precision preserved: whole file folds one table reading lines row-binding:
code: # A line as it would appear in a recorded NDJSON file. line <- paste0(   '{"MessageType":"PositionReport",',   '"MetaData":{"MMSI":368207620,"ShipName":"OCEAN TITAN  ",',   '"latitude":40.12,"longitude":-74.21,',   '"time_utc":"2022-12-29 18:22:32.318353 +0000 UTC"},',   '"Message":{"PositionReport":{"Sog":12.3,"Cog":89.1,',   '"TrueHeading":90,"NavigationalStatus":0,"RateOfTurn":-2}}}' )  parsed <- parse_ais(line) as_position_report(parsed) #>      message_type      mmsi   ship_name latitude longitude            time_utc #>            <char>    <char>      <char>    <num>     <num>              <POSc> #> 1: PositionReport 368207620 OCEAN TITAN    40.12    -74.21 2022-12-29 18:22:32 #>      sog   cog true_heading nav_status rate_of_turn #>    <num> <num>        <num>      <num>        <num> #> 1:  12.3  89.1           90          0           -2 parse_go_time("2022-12-29 18:22:32.318353 +0000 UTC") #> [1] "2022-12-29 18:22:32 UTC" lines <- readLines("ais-data/ais-2024-03-15T09.ndjson") positions <- data.table::rbindlist(   lapply(lines, function(l) as_position_report(parse_ais(l))),   fill = TRUE )

title: Recording AIS
text: re-sent subscription full swap--replace (merge), rate-limited second. update_subscription() replaces stored parameters , socket open, re-sends immediately: whole pattern: subscribe bounding boxes, record raw frames fast, parse offline. connection lifecycle takes care .
code: ais$update_subscription(   bounding_boxes = list(list(min_lat = 50, min_lon = -1.5, max_lat = 51, max_lon = 1.5)) )

what: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: thin R client AISStream.io live vessel-tracking (AIS) WebSocket feed, built connectcore. Open one socket, subscribe bounding boxes plus optional MMSI / message-type filters, handle raw frames Node-ws style. Reconnect, re-subscribe, keepalive silence watchdog inherited; parse helpers durable NDJSON recorder included.

what: Why it is shaped this way
title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: AISStream WebSocket-(REST). connect must send one JSON subscription within three seconds server drops , — rule shapes everything — monitors TCP read queue closes connection backs . whole-world feed runs roughly 300 messages second, message hot path afford per-message JSON parse. aisstream keeps data path parse-free: .dispatch() emits frame raw string cheap prefix check split error frames. record raw frames now (see ndjson_sink()) parse offline. connection machinery — 3-second subscribe, reconnect-- resubscribe, keepalive, silence watchdog — comes connectcore.

title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: package uses renv:
code: renv::install("dereckscompany/aisstream")

what: Constructing a client and inspecting the subscription
title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: Everything network-free — socket opened. build client look exact subscription frame send. Filters omitted entirely empty:
code: library(aisstream)  ais <- AisStream$new(   api_key = "DEMO-KEY", # normally read from the AISSTREAM_API_KEY env var   bounding_boxes = list(     # a box is two opposite [lat, lon] corners; pass it JS-object style ...     list(min_lat = 40.4, min_lon = -74.3, max_lat = 41.0, max_lon = -73.7),     # ... or as raw nested corners (order does not matter).     list(c(50.0, -1.5), c(51.0, 1.5))   ),   message_types = "PositionReport",   ship_mmsi = c("366998510", "367123450") )  # The exact JSON sent on (re)connect — APIKey + BoundingBoxes always, filters only # when non-empty (an empty filter is omitted, never sent as []). cat(ais$subscription_frame()) #> {"APIKey":"DEMO-KEY","BoundingBoxes":[[[40.4,-74.3],[41,-73.7]],[[50,-1.5],[51,1.5]]],"FilterMessageTypes":["PositionReport"],"FiltersShipMMSI":["366998510","367123450"]} minimal <- AisStream$new(   api_key = "DEMO-KEY",   bounding_boxes = list(list(min_lat = -90, min_lon = -180, max_lat = 90, max_lon = 180)) ) cat(minimal$subscription_frame()) #> {"APIKey":"DEMO-KEY","BoundingBoxes":[[[-90,-180],[90,180]]]}

title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: Register handlers Node-ws style, $run() pump event loop. "message" carries raw string (parse must); "error" carries AISStream error frame.
code: ais$on("message", function(raw) {   pr <- as_position_report(parse_ais(raw))   print(pr[, .(mmsi, ship_name, latitude, longitude, sog, cog)]) }) ais$on("error", function(raw) message("AIS error: ", raw))  ais$run() # blocks, pumping the loop; Ctrl-C to stop

title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: parse_ais() JSON.parse AIS, flatteners pull stable common fields data.table. time_utc field arrives Go’s (non-ISO) format parsed specially.
code: frame <- paste0(   '{"MessageType":"PositionReport",',   '"MetaData":{"MMSI":368207620,"ShipName":"OCEAN TITAN  ",',   '"latitude":40.12,"longitude":-74.21,',   '"time_utc":"2022-12-29 18:22:32.318353 +0000 UTC"},',   '"Message":{"PositionReport":{"Sog":12.3,"Cog":89.1,',   '"TrueHeading":90,"NavigationalStatus":0,"RateOfTurn":-2}}}' )  as_position_report(parse_ais(frame)) #>      message_type      mmsi   ship_name latitude longitude            time_utc #>            <char>    <char>      <char>    <num>     <num>              <POSc> #> 1: PositionReport 368207620 OCEAN TITAN    40.12    -74.21 2022-12-29 18:22:32 #>      sog   cog true_heading nav_status rate_of_turn #>    <num> <num>        <num>      <num>        <num> #> 1:  12.3  89.1           90          0           -2

title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: proven “read fast get dropped” pattern: ndjson_sink() returns "message" handler register Node-ws style, run. appends raw frame hourly NDJSON file (rolling UTC hour, flushing every seconds) minimum hot path. See vignette("recording-ais") full rationale.
code: ais <- AisStream$new(   bounding_boxes = list(list(min_lat = -90, min_lon = -180, max_lat = 90, max_lon = 180)),   message_types = "PositionReport" ) ais$on("message", ndjson_sink(dir = "ais-data")) ais$run()

title: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io
text: MIT

title: AIS Message Types — AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES
text: 25 AIS message-type names AISStream.io can deliver. Use build optional FilterMessageTypes subscription field — values must unique subset set (duplicate server error). also keys find incoming frame's MessageType field single key Message object. Reference e.g. AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES[["PositionReport"]].

title: AIS Message Types — AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES
code: AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES

what: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: event-driven client AISStream.io live AIS (vessel-tracking) WebSocket feed, built connectcore::StreamClient. construct API key one bounding boxes (plus optional MMSI / message-type filters), register handlers $(event, handler) exactly Node's ws.(...), $run() pump loop. 3-second subscribe deadline, reconnect--resubscribe, keepalive, silence watchdog inherited.

what: The hot path is parse-free (this is non-negotiable)
title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: AISStream monitors TCP read queue closes connection backs — whole-world firehose (~300 msg/s) per-message JSON parse keep . .dispatch() parsing data path: emits raw string "message", inspection cheap startsWith() prefix check route error frames ({"error": ...}) "error" event instead. Parse handler must, firehose proven pattern ndjson_sink() — $("message", ndjson_sink(dir)) (append raw frames now, parse offline later parse_ais()).

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: box two opposite [lat, lon] corners. Pass either named lists — list(min_lat =, min_lon =, max_lat =, max_lon =) (JS-object style) — raw nested corners list(c(lat1, lon1), c(lat2, lon2)); normalise AISStream's [[min_lat, min_lon], [max_lat, max_lon]]. Latitudes validated [-90, 90], longitudes [-180, 180]. Boxes may overlap.

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: Initialise AisStream client Validates stores subscription parameters, constructs underlying connectcore::StreamClient AISStream endpoint. subscription sent .resubscribe() (re)connect, 3-second subscribe deadline met automatically.

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
code: AisStream$new(   api_key = connectcore::env_or("AISSTREAM_API_KEY"),   bounding_boxes,   message_types = NULL,   ship_mmsi = NULL,   stale_timeout = 120,   ... )

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: api_key (scalar<character>) AISStream.io API key. Defaults AISSTREAM_API_KEY environment variable. bounding_boxes (list) non-empty list bounding boxes, either named list(min_lat =, min_lon =, max_lat =, max_lon =) raw nested corners list(c(lat1, lon1), c(lat2, lon2)). message_types (vector<character, 0..> | NULL) optional FilterMessageTypes; unique values, one AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES. NULL (default) subscribes types. ship_mmsi (vector<character, 0..> | NULL) optional FiltersShipMMSI; 50 MMSI strings. NULL (default) applies MMSI filter. stale_timeout (scalar<numeric ]0, Inf[>) force reconnect frame arrives within many seconds (silence watchdog). Default 120. ... arguments passed connectcore::StreamClient's constructor (e.g. auto_reconnect, max_reconnects, proactive_reconnect).

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: (class<AisStream>) invisibly, self.

title: AisStream: Live Vessel-Tracking Client for AISStream.io — AisStream
text: Replace live subscription (swap--replace) Updates stored subscription parameters , socket open, re-sends new subscription frame immediately. AISStream treats re-sent subscription full swap--replace (merge), rate-limits roughly per second — call second. argument left NULL leaves part subscription unchanged.

what: Flatten an AIS frame's MetaData to a one-row data.table — ais_metadata
title: Flatten an AIS frame's MetaData to a one-row data.table — ais_metadata
text: Pulls stable common fields parsed frame's MetaData (capital D) single tidy row: message_type, mmsi, ship_name (trimmed), latitude, longitude, time_utc (POSIXct, via parse_go_time()). Present every message type, safe denominator across feed.

title: Flatten an AIS frame's MetaData to a one-row data.table — ais_metadata
code: ais_metadata(parsed)

title: Flatten an AIS frame's MetaData to a one-row data.table — ais_metadata
text: parsed (list) one frame, already parsed parse_ais().

title: Flatten an AIS frame's MetaData to a one-row data.table — ais_metadata
text: (data.table) one-row data.table common metadata. message_type (character | NA) AIS message type (e.g. "PositionReport"); NA frame omits . mmsi (character | NA) vessel MMSI identifier; NA absent. ship_name (character | NA) trimmed ship name; NA absent. latitude (numeric | NA) latitude decimal degrees; NA absent. longitude (numeric | NA) longitude decimal degrees; NA absent. time_utc (POSIXct | NA) frame timestamp UTC; NA missing unparseable.

what: aisstream: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io — aisstream-package
title: aisstream: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io — aisstream-package
text: thin R client AISStream.io live vessel-tracking WebSocket feed, built connectcore. Open one socket, subscribe bounding boxes plus optional MMSI / message-type filters, handle raw frames Node-ws style. Reconnect, re-subscribe, keepalive watchdog inherited; message hot path parse-free vendor never drops slow reader, parse helpers plus durable NDJSON recorder included.

title: aisstream: Live Vessel-Tracking Stream Client for AISStream.io — aisstream-package
text: Maintainer: Dereck Mezquita dereck@mezquita.io (ORCID) Authors: Dereck Mezquita dereck@mezquita.io (ORCID)

what: Flatten an AIS PositionReport to a one-row data.table — as_position_report
title: Flatten an AIS PositionReport to a one-row data.table — as_position_report
text: Combines common metadata (see ais_metadata()) stable fields PositionReport body — speed/course ground, true heading, navigational status, rate turn — one tidy row. Returns metadata row NA position fields frame PositionReport (row-binds cleanly across mixed stream). body lives Message$PositionReport.

title: Flatten an AIS PositionReport to a one-row data.table — as_position_report
code: as_position_report(parsed)

title: Flatten an AIS PositionReport to a one-row data.table — as_position_report
text: parsed (list) one frame, already parsed parse_ais().

title: Flatten an AIS PositionReport to a one-row data.table — as_position_report
text: (data.table) one-row data.table position fields plus metadata. message_type (character | NA) AIS message type (e.g. "PositionReport"); NA frame omits . mmsi (character | NA) vessel MMSI identifier; NA absent. ship_name (character | NA) trimmed ship name; NA absent. latitude (numeric | NA) latitude decimal degrees; NA absent. longitude (numeric | NA) longitude decimal degrees; NA absent. time_utc (POSIXct | NA) frame timestamp UTC; NA missing unparseable. sog (numeric | NA) speed ground (knots); NA non-position frame. cog (numeric | NA) course ground (degrees); NA non-position frame. true_heading (numeric | NA) true heading (degrees); NA non-position frame. nav_status (numeric | NA) navigational status code; NA non-position frame. rate_of_turn (numeric | NA) rate turn; NA non-position frame.

what: Build the AISStream.io subscription frame (as a JSON string) — build_subscription
title: Build the AISStream.io subscription frame (as a JSON string) — build_subscription
text: Assembles one subscription message AISStream expects within 3 seconds connecting: APIKey BoundingBoxes always present; FilterMessageTypes FiltersShipMMSI included non-empty (server rejects empty []). Bounding boxes normalised range-checked, MMSI filter capped 50, message types must unique subset AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES. auto_unbox = TRUE keeps API key scalar fields JSON scalars, box/filter arrays stay arrays.

title: Build the AISStream.io subscription frame (as a JSON string) — build_subscription
code: build_subscription(   api_key,   bounding_boxes,   message_types = NULL,   ship_mmsi = NULL )

title: Build the AISStream.io subscription frame (as a JSON string) — build_subscription
text: api_key (scalar<character>) AISStream.io API key (non-empty). bounding_boxes (list) non-empty list boxes (see normalise_bounding_boxes()). message_types (vector<character, 0..> | NULL) optional FilterMessageTypes; unique values, one AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES. NULL/empty omits field. ship_mmsi (vector<character, 0..> | NULL) optional FiltersShipMMSI; 50 MMSI strings. NULL/empty omits field.

title: Build the AISStream.io subscription frame (as a JSON string) — build_subscription
text: (scalar<character>) subscription frame JSON string.

title: Build the hourly NDJSON file path for a UTC time — ndjson_hour_path

title: Build the hourly NDJSON file path for a UTC time — ndjson_hour_path
code: ndjson_hour_path(dir, at = lubridate::now("UTC"))

title: Build the hourly NDJSON file path for a UTC time — ndjson_hour_path
text: dir (scalar<character>) output directory. (class<POSIXct>) time whose hour names file. Default lubridate::now("UTC").

title: Build the hourly NDJSON file path for a UTC time — ndjson_hour_path
text: (scalar<character>) full file path hour.

what: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
title: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
text: proven "read fast get dropped" recorder, message-handler factory. Returns function(raw) register — ais$("message", ndjson_sink(dir)) — exactly way use connectcore::ws_file_sink(), sink pure handler dependency client reads like Node.

title: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
code: ndjson_sink(dir, flush_seconds = 5)

title: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
text: dir (scalar<character>) output directory; created missing. flush_seconds (scalar<numeric ]0, Inf[>) minimum seconds flushes disk; data--risk window abrupt kill. Default 5.

title: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
text: (function) handler function(raw) suitable ais$("message", ...).

title: A durable hourly-NDJSON message sink — ndjson_sink
text: frame keeps durable-write behaviour message-driven (separate later timer): every call computes current UTC hour slot , slot changes (first frame), closes previous hourly file opens next one (<dir>/ais-YYYY-MM-DDTHH.ndjson) append mode; appends raw frame plus newline immediately — hot path stays parse-free, one frame per line (NDJSON). least flush_seconds elapsed since last flush flush() connection reset timer. Durability semantics: writes append-, already-flushed line survives abrupt kill (SIGKILL, power loss); data risk bounded frames written last flush_seconds window. final partial buffer flushed process exits — open file connection flushed closed garbage collection / R shutdown — clean stop loses nothing. Parse resulting files offline parse_ais() flatteners.

what: Normalise a single bounding box to AISStream's nested-corner form — normalise_bounding_box
title: Normalise a single bounding box to AISStream's nested-corner form — normalise_bounding_box
text: Accepts box either supported shape returns AISStream's canonical list(c(min_lat, min_lon), c(max_lat, max_lon)) (two [lat, lon] corners): named list list(min_lat=, min_lon=, max_lat=, max_lon=) (JS-object style), raw nested box list(c(lat1, lon1), c(lat2, lon2)) (corner order irrelevant; corners sorted min/max pair). Every latitude validated [-90, 90] every longitude [-180, 180].

title: Normalise a single bounding box to AISStream's nested-corner form — normalise_bounding_box
code: normalise_bounding_box(box)

title: Normalise a single bounding box to AISStream's nested-corner form — normalise_bounding_box
text: box (list) one bounding box, either supported shape.

title: Normalise a single bounding box to AISStream's nested-corner form — normalise_bounding_box
text: (list) box list(c(min_lat, min_lon), c(max_lat, max_lon)).

title: Normalise a list of bounding boxes — normalise_bounding_boxes
text: Applies normalise_bounding_box() box non-empty list; boxes may overlap (AISStream de-duplicates server-side).

code: normalise_bounding_boxes(bounding_boxes)

text: bounding_boxes (list) non-empty list boxes, shape normalise_bounding_box() accepts.

title: Normalise a list of bounding boxes — normalise_bounding_boxes
text: (list) list canonical nested-corner boxes.

what: Parse a raw AIS frame (JSON.parse for AIS) — parse_ais
title: Parse a raw AIS frame (JSON.parse for AIS) — parse_ais
text: thin wrapper jsonlite::fromJSON() (simplifyVector = FALSE, shape predictable nested list) — AIS analogue JavaScript's JSON.parse. Deliberately generic: model 25 message bodies, AISStream API BETA bodies shift. Use handler, — typically — offline recorded NDJSON. Error frames ({"error": "..."}) parse like object.

title: Parse a raw AIS frame (JSON.parse for AIS) — parse_ais
code: parse_ais(raw)

title: Parse a raw AIS frame (JSON.parse for AIS) — parse_ais
text: raw (scalar<character>) one raw frame JSON string.

title: Parse a raw AIS frame (JSON.parse for AIS) — parse_ais
text: (list) parsed frame (nested list).

what: Parse a Go-format AIS timestamp to POSIXct (UTC) — parse_go_time
title: Parse a Go-format AIS timestamp to POSIXct (UTC) — parse_go_time
text: AISStream stamps every frame's MetaData$time_utc Go's default time format — "2006-01-02 15:04:05.999999999 -0700 MST", e.g. "2022-12-29 18:22:32.318353 +0000 UTC" — ISO 8601, plain parser fails. strips trailing zone name (UTC) parses rest "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS %z", preserving sub-second precision. Returns NA (UTC) NULL/empty/unparseable input rather erroring.

title: Parse a Go-format AIS timestamp to POSIXct (UTC) — parse_go_time
code: parse_go_time(x)

title: Parse a Go-format AIS timestamp to POSIXct (UTC) — parse_go_time
text: x () scalar timestamp string (NULL).

title: Parse a Go-format AIS timestamp to POSIXct (UTC) — parse_go_time
text: (class<POSIXct>) time UTC (length 1; NA unparseable).

title: aisstream 0.0.3
text: data.table shapes now documented per column. @return ais_metadata() as_position_report() now refines data.table typed nested bullets — one bullet per column carrying roxyassert element type (character | NA, numeric | NA, POSIXct | NA) — instead bare prose description. Documenting columns generates matching assert_has_columns() per-column contracts, flattener emits wrong column set type now caught boundary. Every column nullable derives *_or_na coercion (parse_go_time(), yields NA missing unparseable timestamp).

title: aisstream 0.0.2
text: Recorder API reshaped: record_to_ndjson() → ndjson_sink(). recorder now message-handler factory register , instead free function mutated client side effect: ndjson_sink(dir, flush_seconds = 5) returns function(raw) , frame, appends raw string current hourly file, rolls file turn UTC hour, flushes per flush_seconds — message-driven, separate later timer dependency client. Durability unchanged: durable write , rotation (ais-YYYY-MM-DDTHH.ndjson via ndjson_hour_path(), kept), data--risk abrupt kill still bounded roughly last flush_seconds. breaking change, made deliberately pre-1.0 (package zero adoption, deprecation shim). motivation idiom: sink now mirrors connectcore::ws_file_sink() (factory returns handler) reads like Node — ais$("message", ...) — rather free function reaching client wire handlers timer. unused later import dropped (sink longer schedules later loop).
code: # before record_to_ndjson(ais, dir = "ais-data"); ais$run() # after ais$on("message", ndjson_sink("ais-data")); ais$run()

title: aisstream 0.0.1
text: Initial release. thin R client AISStream.io live vessel-tracking (AIS) WebSocket feed, built connectcore. Open one socket, subscribe bounding boxes plus optional MMSI / message-type filters, handle raw frames Node-ws style — reconnect, re-subscribe, keepalive silence watchdog inherited connectcore::StreamClient. AisStream — client (R6 subclass connectcore::StreamClient). Construct API key bounding boxes, register handlers $(event, handler), $run(). overrides two seams: .resubscribe() (send subscription frame every (re)connect, meeting server’s 3-second subscribe deadline) .dispatch() (route {"error": ...} frames "error" event). $update_subscription() swaps live subscription (swap--replace, ~1/sec). hot path parse-free. AISStream closes connection whose TCP read queue backs , whole-world firehose runs ~300 msg/s — .dispatch() JSON parsing data path. emits raw string "message"; inspection cheap prefix check split error frames. record_to_ndjson() — durable “read fast get dropped” recorder. Opens hourly NDJSON file frame, appends raw frame minimal work, flushes / rolls UTC hour later loop. Parse files offline. Parse helpers — parse_ais() (JSON.parse AIS), parse_go_time() (Go non-ISO time_utc format), flatteners ais_metadata() / as_position_report() stable common fields, plus bounding-box subscription builders. AIS_MESSAGE_TYPES exports 25 known type names. Every argument return typed runtime-checked roxyassert.
